label: Inloggning — Clerk
summary: Drop-in user authentication via Clerk — the default provider under the 'auth' capability (supabase-auth is the sibling for explicit Supabase asks). Adds the <ClerkProvider /> root-layout wrapper, route-protecting middleware, and <SignInButton /> / <UserButton /> primitives. Demo mode (mock: visual): with missing/placeholder keys the same sign-in/sign-up buttons render but open an honest demo dialog — no fake sessions, no crash; real Clerk activates once keys are stored. The middleware MUST stay verbatim (matcher syntax and route ordering are load-bearing).
summarySv: Inloggning och konton via Clerk (standardvalet för inloggning). Utan riktiga nycklar visas inloggningsknapparna i demoläge: besökaren ser flödet men ingen riktig inloggning sker — en tydlig demoruta förklarar hur Clerk kopplas in. Med nycklar på plats blir samma knappar riktig inloggning.

envVars.purpose: Server-side Clerk API authentication. Clerk dashboard → API Keys → Secret keys (sk_test_/sk_live_). Tagged 'build' because auth is non-functional without it. The middleware key-gates itself: missing/placeholder keys degrade to open routes (NextResponse.next()) instead of crashing every request.

Client-side Clerk SDK init. Clerk dashboard → API Keys → Publishable keys (pk_test_/pk_live_). Tagged 'build' because auth is non-functional without it. ClerkProviderShell validates the key structurally and renders an 'Auth not configured' banner on missing/placeholder values instead of letting <ClerkProvider> throw.
Optional override for the sign-in route path. Defaults to '/sign-in' when unset. Only set this if the project mounts the Clerk sign-in component on a non-default path (e.g. '/login').
Optional override for the sign-up route path. Defaults to '/sign-up' when unset. Only set this if the project uses a different signup path (e.g. '/register').
